things.name: Old Havana (Habana Vieja)
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage colonial city centre — baroque cathedrals, plazas, Hemingway's bars and vintage cars

things.name: Malecón Seawall
things.description: Havana's famous 8-km seafront boulevard — social heart of the city at sunset with music and conversation

things.name: Tropicana Cabaret
things.description: The world's most famous open-air cabaret — spectacular costume shows under the stars since 1939

things.name: Vintage Car Tour
things.description: Cruise Havana in a 1950s American convertible — Buicks, Chevrolets and Fords in every pastel colour

things.name: Hemingway's Havana Trail
things.description: Daiquiris at El Floridita, mojitos at La Bodeguita del Medio — and a visit to Finca Vigía, his home

things.name: Museum of the Revolution
things.description: Batista's former presidential palace tells the story of the Cuban Revolution — the Granma yacht is outside

things.name: Viñales Valley Day Trip
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage tobacco valley with spectacular mogote rock formations — 3 hours west of Havana

things.name: Fusterlandia Jaimanitas
things.description: Extraordinary neighbourhood transformed by artist José Fuster into a Gaudí-inspired mosaic fantasy

things.name: Callejón de Hamel
things.description: Afro-Cuban cultural alley — Sunday rumba performances, murals and santería ceremonies

things.name: Morro Castle (Castillo del Morro)
things.description: 16th-century fortress guarding the entrance to Havana harbour — cannon-firing ceremony at sunset
